observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:

c. That all contracts for the procurement of goods should be subjected to public bidding to ensure that the most advantageous price is obtained from qualified bidder.

We have recommended to the head of the procuring entity to submit the required documents. We also recommend that the agency officials, particularly the budget officer, accountant, and treasurer who certifies to the existence of appropriation, the completeness of supporting documents and the availability of funds respectively to carefully examine all the supporting documents before payment will be effected to avoid accountability in the performance of official duties and functions.

 We have also recommended to the Municipal Budget Officer to submit proof that the payment of the loan amortization is provided in the Approved Annual Budget.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Treasurer the following:

submit the Report of Collections and Deposits together with the Official Receipts from the month of December 2017 to August 2019 to the Audit Team for verification.

Submit the required reports on time and reconcile the collections per Official Receipts with per Report of Collections and Deposits.

Explain in writing why the later reports were submitted and earlier reports were not submitted until to date.

We have recommended the following:

The Municipal Accountant-Designate to prepare monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement for all of its depository accounts and submit them to the Office of the Auditor within fifteen (15) days after the end of the month to establish the adjusted balance of cash-in bank account every as of the month end; and
The Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Treasurer as signatories of the bank accounts of the Municipality to authorize the Municipal Accountant to enroll on e-access of their accounts so that bank statements could readily be available at any time.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit Report of Disbursements in the liquidation of his cash advances, and submit the same together with the supporting documents to the Office of the Municipal Accountant;
Direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to check completeness of liquidation documents before recording in the books of accounts as fully liquidated. Liquidations with insufficient supporting documents should not be received, but should be returned back to the Municipal Treasurer and/or concerned personnel for completion. If complete, submit the Report of Disbursements with all the vouchers/payrolls and supporting documents to the Auditor within twenty (20) days from the accomplishment of the purpose pursuant to Section 1 of COA Circular 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012; and
Direct the Municipal Treasurer and concerned personnel to strictly comply with the timeline of the liquidation of cash advances for payroll, operating expenses and travel as provided in Section 5.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:

Create an inventory committee to conduct an actual physical inventory taking of all properties, plant and equipment headed by the General Services Officer to be witness by any representative of the Commission on Audit.

Require the General Service Officer to maintain stock cards of each property, plant and equipment bearing property number while the Municipal Accountant should maintain a subsidiary ledger card on each account.

Require the General Services Officer to identify unserviceable properties and prepare an Inspection and Inventory Report for disposal.

Required the Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary adjustments on the deficiencies noted in the course of inventory taking and make reconciliation with inventory report, stock cards and subsidiary ledgers.

Direct the Municipal Administrator to update the list of properties that need to be insured and submit the list to the GSIS.

Direct the Municipal Officer to provide budget for the insurance premium of properties identified to be insured especially building to protect the interest of the government.

Require the Municipal Accountant to compute a yearly depreciation of all depreciable properties and make the necessary adjustment in the books based on the computation.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Treasurer the following:

Deposit the amount of P562,962.68 representing undeposited collections from September 2019 to December, 2019;

Strictly follow the COA Circular No. 92-382.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to see to it that cash advances are supported with the necessary documents, and additional cash advance for the same purpose shall be granted after previous cash advance is liquidated, or proper accounting thereof is made.

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to stop the payment of reimbursements through his cash advance, reimbursement is discouraged as mode of payment for LGU’s expenses; and also, payment to suppliers is made thru checks. And follow Republic Act 9184 in all procurement of goods.
